speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Call forth Bagot.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Now, Bagot, freely speak thy mind

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: What thou dost know of noble Gloucester’s death,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Who wrought it with the King, and who performed

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The bloody office of his timeless end.

speaker: BAGOT
text: Then set before my face the Lord Aumerle.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Cousin, stand forth, and look upon that man.

speaker: BAGOT
text: My Lord Aumerle, I know your daring tongue

speaker: BAGOT
text: Scorns to unsay what once it hath delivered.

speaker: BAGOT
text: In that dead time when Gloucester’s death was plotted,

speaker: BAGOT
text: I heard you say “Is not my arm of length,

speaker: BAGOT
text: That reacheth from the restful English court

speaker: BAGOT
text: As far as Calais, to mine uncle’s head?”

speaker: BAGOT
text: Amongst much other talk that very time

speaker: BAGOT
text: I heard you say that you had rather refuse

speaker: BAGOT
text: The offer of an hundred thousand crowns

speaker: BAGOT
text: Than Bolingbroke’s return to England,

speaker: BAGOT
text: Adding withal how blest this land would be

speaker: BAGOT
text: In this your cousin’s death.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Princes and noble lords,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: What answer shall I make to this base man?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Shall I so much dishonor my fair stars

speaker: AUMERLE
text: On equal terms to give him chastisement?

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Either I must or have mine honor soiled

speaker: AUMERLE
text: With the attainder of his slanderous lips.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: There is my gage, the manual seal of death

speaker: AUMERLE
text: That marks thee out for hell. I say thou liest,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: And will maintain what thou hast said is false

speaker: AUMERLE
text: In thy heart-blood, though being all too base

speaker: AUMERLE
text: To stain the temper of my knightly sword.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Bagot, forbear. Thou shalt not take it up.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Excepting one, I would he were the best

speaker: AUMERLE
text: In all this presence that hath moved me so.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: If that thy valor stand on sympathy,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: There is my gage, Aumerle, in gage to thine.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: By that fair sun which shows me where thou stand’st,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: I heard thee say, and vauntingly thou spak’st it,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: That thou wert cause of noble Gloucester’s death.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: If thou deniest it twenty times, thou liest,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: And I will turn thy falsehood to thy heart,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Where it was forgèd, with my rapier’s point.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Thou dar’st not, coward, live to see that day.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Now, by my soul, I would it were this hour.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Fitzwater, thou art damned to hell for this.

speaker: PERCY
text: Aumerle, thou liest! His honor is as true

speaker: PERCY
text: In this appeal as thou art all unjust;

speaker: PERCY
text: And that thou art so, there I throw my gage,

speaker: PERCY
text: To prove it on thee to the extremest point

speaker: PERCY
text: Of mortal breathing. Seize it if thou dar’st.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: An if I do not, may my hands rot off

speaker: AUMERLE
text: And never brandish more revengeful steel

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Over the glittering helmet of my foe!

speaker:
ANOTHER
               
              LORD
text: I task the earth to the like, forsworn Aumerle,

speaker:
ANOTHER
               
              LORD
text: And spur thee on with full as many lies

speaker:
ANOTHER
               
              LORD
text: As may be holloed in thy treacherous ear

speaker:
ANOTHER
               
              LORD
text: From sun to sun. There is my honor’s pawn.

speaker:
ANOTHER
               
              LORD
text: Engage it to the trial if thou darest.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Who sets me else? By heaven, I’ll throw at all!

speaker: AUMERLE
text: I have a thousand spirits in one breast

speaker: AUMERLE
text: To answer twenty thousand such as you.

speaker: SURREY
text: My Lord Fitzwater, I do remember well

speaker: SURREY
text: The very time Aumerle and you did talk.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: ’Tis very true. You were in presence then,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: And you can witness with me this is true.

speaker: SURREY
text: As false, by heaven, as heaven itself is true.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Surrey, thou liest.

speaker: SURREY
text: Dishonorable boy,

speaker: SURREY
text: That lie shall lie so heavy on my sword

speaker: SURREY
text: That it shall render vengeance and revenge

speaker: SURREY
text: Till thou the lie-giver and that lie do lie

speaker: SURREY
text: In earth as quiet as thy father’s skull.

speaker: SURREY
text: In proof whereof, there is my honor’s pawn.

speaker: SURREY
text: Engage it to the trial if thou dar’st.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: How fondly dost thou spur a forward horse!

speaker: FITZWATER
text: If I dare eat or drink or breathe or live,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: I dare meet Surrey in a wilderness

speaker: FITZWATER
text: And spit upon him whilst I say he lies,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: And lies, and lies. There is my bond of faith

speaker: FITZWATER
text: To tie thee to my strong correction.

speaker: FITZWATER
text: As I intend to thrive in this new world,

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Aumerle is guilty of my true appeal.—

speaker: FITZWATER
text: Besides, I heard the banished Norfolk say

speaker: FITZWATER
text: That thou, Aumerle, didst send two of thy men

speaker: FITZWATER
text: To execute the noble duke at Calais.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: Some honest Christian trust me with a gage.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: That Norfolk lies, here do I throw down this,

speaker: AUMERLE
text: If he may be repealed to try his honor.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: These differences shall all rest under gage

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Till Norfolk be repealed. Repealed he shall be,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And though mine enemy, restored again

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: To all his lands and seigniories. When he is returned,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Against Aumerle we will enforce his trial.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: That honorable day shall never be seen.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Many a time hath banished Norfolk fought

speaker: CARLISLE
text: For Jesu Christ in glorious Christian field,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Streaming the ensign of the Christian cross

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Against black pagans, Turks, and Saracens;

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And, toiled with works of war, retired himself

speaker: CARLISLE
text: To Italy, and there at Venice gave

speaker: CARLISLE
text: His body to that pleasant country’s earth

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And his pure soul unto his captain, Christ,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Under whose colors he had fought so long.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Why, bishop, is Norfolk dead?

speaker: CARLISLE
text: As surely as I live, my lord.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Sweet peace conduct his sweet soul to the bosom

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Of good old Abraham! Lords appellants,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Your differences shall all rest under gage

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Till we assign you to your days of trial.

speaker: YORK
text: Great Duke of Lancaster, I come to thee

speaker: YORK
text: From plume-plucked Richard, who with willing soul

speaker: YORK
text: Adopts thee heir, and his high scepter yields

speaker: YORK
text: To the possession of thy royal hand.

speaker: YORK
text: Ascend his throne, descending now from him,

speaker: YORK
text: And long live Henry, fourth of that name!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: In God’s name, I’ll ascend the regal throne.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Marry, God forbid!

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Worst in this royal presence may I speak,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Yet best beseeming me to speak the truth.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Would God that any in this noble presence

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Were enough noble to be upright judge

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Of noble Richard! Then true noblesse would

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Learn him forbearance from so foul a wrong.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: What subject can give sentence on his king?

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And who sits here that is not Richard’s subject?

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Thieves are not judged but they are by to hear,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Although apparent guilt be seen in them;

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And shall the figure of God’s majesty,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: His captain, steward, deputy elect,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Anointed, crowned, planted many years,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Be judged by subject and inferior breath,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And he himself not present? O, forfend it God

speaker: CARLISLE
text: That in a Christian climate souls refined

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Should show so heinous, black, obscene a deed!

speaker: CARLISLE
text: I speak to subjects and a subject speaks,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Stirred up by God thus boldly for his king.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: My Lord of Hereford here, whom you call king,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Is a foul traitor to proud Hereford’s king,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And if you crown him, let me prophesy

speaker: CARLISLE
text: The blood of English shall manure the ground

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And future ages groan for this foul act,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Peace shall go sleep with Turks and infidels,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: And in this seat of peace tumultuous wars

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Shall kin with kin and kind with kind confound.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Disorder, horror, fear, and mutiny

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Shall here inhabit, and this land be called

speaker: CARLISLE
text: The field of Golgotha and dead men’s skulls.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: O, if you raise this house against this house,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: It will the woefullest division prove

speaker: CARLISLE
text: That ever fell upon this cursèd earth!

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Prevent it, resist it, let it not be so,

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Lest child, child’s children, cry against you woe!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Well have you argued, sir, and, for your pains,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Of capital treason we arrest you here.—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My Lord of Westminster, be it your charge

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: To keep him safely till his day of trial.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: May it please you, lords, to grant the commons’ suit?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Fetch hither Richard, that in common view

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: He may surrender. So we shall proceed

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Without suspicion.

speaker: YORK
text: I will be his conduct.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Lords, you that here are under our arrest,

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Procure your sureties for your days of answer.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Little are we beholding to your love

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: And little looked for at your helping hands.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Alack, why am I sent for to a king

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Before I have shook off the regal thoughts

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Wherewith I reigned? I hardly yet have learned

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To insinuate, flatter, bow, and bend my knee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Give sorrow leave awhile to tutor me

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To this submission. Yet I well remember

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The favors of these men. Were they not mine?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Did they not sometime cry “All hail” to me?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So Judas did to Christ, but He in twelve

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Found truth in all but one; I, in twelve thousand, none.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God save the King! Will no man say “amen”?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Am I both priest and clerk? Well, then, amen.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God save the King, although I be not he,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And yet amen, if heaven do think him me.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To do what service am I sent for hither?

speaker: YORK
text: To do that office of thine own goodwill

speaker: YORK
text: Which tired majesty did make thee offer:

speaker: YORK
text: The resignation of thy state and crown

speaker: YORK
text: To Henry Bolingbroke.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Give me the crown.—Here, cousin, seize the crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Here, cousin.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: On this side my hand, on that side thine.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Now is this golden crown like a deep well

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That owes two buckets, filling one another,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The emptier ever dancing in the air,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The other down, unseen, and full of water.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That bucket down and full of tears am I,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Drinking my griefs, whilst you mount up on high.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: I thought you had been willing to resign.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My crown I am, but still my griefs are mine.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: You may my glories and my state depose

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But not my griefs; still am I king of those.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Part of your cares you give me with your crown.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Your cares set up do not pluck my cares down.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My care is loss of care, by old care done;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Your care is gain of care, by new care won.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The cares I give I have, though given away.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: They ’tend the crown, yet still with me they stay.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Are you contented to resign the crown?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Ay, no; no, ay; for I must nothing be.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Therefore no “no,” for I resign to thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Now, mark me how I will undo myself.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I give this heavy weight from off my head

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And this unwieldy scepter from my hand,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The pride of kingly sway from out my heart.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With mine own tears I wash away my balm,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With mine own hands I give away my crown,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With mine own tongue deny my sacred state,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With mine own breath release all duteous oaths.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: All pomp and majesty I do forswear.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My manors, rents, revenues I forgo;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My acts, decrees, and statutes I deny.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God pardon all oaths that are broke to me.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God keep all vows unbroke are made to thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Make me, that nothing have, with nothing grieved,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And thou with all pleased that hast all achieved.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Long mayst thou live in Richard’s seat to sit,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And soon lie Richard in an earthy pit.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: God save King Henry, unkinged Richard says,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And send him many years of sunshine days.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What more remains?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: No more, but that you read

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: These accusations and these grievous crimes

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Committed by your person and your followers

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Against the state and profit of this land;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That, by confessing them, the souls of men

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: May deem that you are worthily deposed.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Must I do so? And must I ravel out

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: My weaved-up follies? Gentle Northumberland,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: If thy offenses were upon record,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Would it not shame thee in so fair a troop

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To read a lecture of them? If thou wouldst,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: There shouldst thou find one heinous article

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Containing the deposing of a king

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And cracking the strong warrant of an oath,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Marked with a blot, damned in the book of heaven.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nay, all of you that stand and look upon me

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whilst that my wretchedness doth bait myself,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Though some of you, with Pilate, wash your hands,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Showing an outward pity, yet you Pilates

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Have here delivered me to my sour cross,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And water cannot wash away your sin.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My lord, dispatch. Read o’er these articles.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Mine eyes are full of tears; I cannot see.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And yet salt water blinds them not so much

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But they can see a sort of traitors here.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nay, if I turn mine eyes upon myself,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I find myself a traitor with the rest,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For I have given here my soul’s consent

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: T’ undeck the pompous body of a king,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Made glory base and sovereignty a slave,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Proud majesty a subject, state a peasant.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My lord—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: No lord of thine, thou haught insulting man,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Nor no man’s lord. I have no name, no title,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: No, not that name was given me at the font,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But ’tis usurped. Alack the heavy day,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That I have worn so many winters out

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And know not now what name to call myself.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: O, that I were a mockery king of snow

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Standing before the sun of Bolingbroke,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To melt myself away in water drops.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Good king, great king, and yet not greatly good,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: An if my word be sterling yet in England,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Let it command a mirror hither straight,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That it may show me what a face I have

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Since it is bankrupt of his majesty.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Go, some of you, and fetch a looking-glass.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Read o’er this paper while the glass doth come.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Fiend, thou torments me ere I come to hell!

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Urge it no more, my Lord Northumberland.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The commons will not then be satisfied.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: They shall be satisfied. I’ll read enough

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: When I do see the very book indeed

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Where all my sins are writ, and that’s myself.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Give me that glass, and therein will I read.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: No deeper wrinkles yet? Hath sorrow struck

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So many blows upon this face of mine

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And made no deeper wounds? O flatt’ring glass,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Like to my followers in prosperity,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thou dost beguile me. Was this face the face

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That every day under his household roof

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Did keep ten thousand men? Was this the face

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That like the sun did make beholders wink?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Is this the face which faced so many follies,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That was at last outfaced by Bolingbroke?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A brittle glory shineth in this face.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As brittle as the glory is the face,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For there it is, cracked in an hundred shivers.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Mark, silent king, the moral of this sport:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: How soon my sorrow hath destroyed my face.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The shadow of your sorrow hath destroyed

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: The shadow of your face.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Say that again.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The shadow of my sorrow? Ha, let’s see.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: ’Tis very true. My grief lies all within;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And these external manners of laments

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Are merely shadows to the unseen grief

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That swells with silence in the tortured soul.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: There lies the substance. And I thank thee, king,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For thy great bounty, that not only giv’st

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Me cause to wail but teachest me the way

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: How to lament the cause. I’ll beg one boon

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And then be gone and trouble you no more.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Shall I obtain it?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Name it, fair cousin.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: “Fair cousin”? I am greater than a king,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For when I was a king, my flatterers

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Were then but subjects. Being now a subject,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I have a king here to my flatterer.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Being so great, I have no need to beg.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Yet ask.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And shall I have?

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: You shall.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Then give me leave to go.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Whither?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whither you will, so I were from your sights.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Go, some of you, convey him to the Tower.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: O, good! “Convey”? Conveyers are you all,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: That rise thus nimbly by a true king’s fall.

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: On Wednesday next, we solemnly set down

speaker: BOLINGBROKE
text: Our coronation. Lords, prepare yourselves.

speaker: ABBOT
text: A woeful pageant have we here beheld.

speaker: CARLISLE
text: The woe’s to come. The children yet unborn

speaker: CARLISLE
text: Shall feel this day as sharp to them as thorn.

speaker: AUMERLE
text: You holy clergymen, is there no plot

speaker: AUMERLE
text: To rid the realm of this pernicious blot?

speaker: ABBOT
text: My lord,

speaker: ABBOT
text: Before I freely speak my mind herein,

speaker: ABBOT
text: You shall not only take the sacrament

speaker: ABBOT
text: To bury mine intents, but also to effect

speaker: ABBOT
text: Whatever I shall happen to devise.

speaker: ABBOT
text: I see your brows are full of discontent,

speaker: ABBOT
text: Your hearts of sorrow, and your eyes of tears.

speaker: ABBOT
text: Come home with me to supper. I’ll lay

speaker: ABBOT
text: A plot shall show us all a merry day.
